initial_non_recommendation_reasons: The supplied brief mixed open proposals, an intent announcement, a preprint, and a changelog declaration with stronger maturity wording.
The proposed work could displace PR-G2A or be misread as authorization to create runtime capabilities.
Identity, access authorization, telemetry, receipts, evidence, verification, and truth were not yet separated by explicit non-promotion rules.

sources.verified_claim: The issue is a meeting template; it does not itself record an adopted agenda, minutes, decision, or standard.
The issue proposes a standardized telemetry schema and explicitly distinguishes telemetry from an authoritative durable audit record.
The issue proposes independently and offline-verifiable decision evidence with decision-time signing, tamper evidence, justification binding, and human-authority context.
The issue discusses a runtime policy and evidence reference implementation; it is not a released reference implementation or adopted control.

Enterprise-Managed Authorization centralizes MCP server access through an enterprise identity provider; it does not prove agent-persona continuity, delegation scope, action reasonableness, or evidence sufficiency.
The announcement describes DNS-based identity, verification, and discovery goals; an intent announcement is not a stable standard or proof of adoption.
A public implementation repository now exists and was active on 2026-07-21; this corrects an intent-only reading but does not establish a stable standard, deployment, or DBA adoption.
The main-branch changelog declares 5.0.0 features including ACS alignment, NonoSandbox, and denylist controls; no v5.0.0 release or tag was found.
The latest published GitHub Release observed on 2026-07-22 is v4.1.0, published 2026-06-09.
LogicHunter reports silent semantic failures and 40 bugs, with 30 confirmed and 26 fixed; the source is a preprint and does not establish peer review or independent replication.
The Europe CFP is closed; notifications are dated 2026-07-21 and the schedule announcement is dated 2026-07-23, so no accepted-program claim is made on 2026-07-22.

corrections.supplied_claim: FINOS issue 348 already provides the substantive meeting agenda for telemetry and offline evidence.
corrections.verified_correction: Issue 348 is an open meeting template with blank agenda and minutes. Issues 346, 341, and 340 are separate open proposals or ideas, not adopted framework controls.

corrections.supplied_claim: Microsoft Agent Governance Toolkit v5.0 is a published release.
corrections.verified_correction: Version 5.0.0 appears in the main-branch changelog, but no v5.0.0 tag or GitHub Release was found; v4.1.0 is the latest published release observed.

corrections.supplied_claim: Agent Name Service remains only an intent announcement without a technical repository.
corrections.verified_correction: The Linux Foundation source remains an intent announcement, but a public and active agentnameservice/ans implementation repository now exists. Neither fact proves a stable standard or adoption.

corrections.supplied_claim: LogicHunter establishes a generally validated defect rate for agent frameworks.
corrections.verified_correction: LogicHunter is an author preprint reporting results for its studied scope; peer review, independent replication, and generalization are not established by the source.

boundary_mappings.dba_route: Existing OpenTelemetry profiles and observation plane
boundary_mappings.allowed_interpretation: A comparison input for event fields, transport neutrality, and receipt linkage.
boundary_mappings.forbidden_promotion: Telemetry must not be promoted to Evidence, durable audit authority, Verification, Truth, Capability, Permission, or gate closure.

boundary_mappings.dba_route: DQ-006 Agent Evidence portfolio review and blocked DQ-021 Telemetry-to-Evidence admission
boundary_mappings.allowed_interpretation: A gap-comparison input for decision-time signing, independent verification, justification binding, and human-authority context.
boundary_mappings.forbidden_promotion: A receipt, signature, hash, or attestation must not be promoted to Truth, Permission, correct execution, evidence sufficiency, or PR-G2 approval.

boundary_mappings.dba_route: External access-authorization input adjacent to ARO and existing identity/permission boundaries
boundary_mappings.allowed_interpretation: A reference for enterprise-controlled MCP server access and identity-provider policy.
boundary_mappings.forbidden_promotion: Access authorization must not be promoted to agent-persona continuity, delegation authority, action reasonableness, Execution, Evidence, or production readiness.

boundary_mappings.external_signal: Agent Name Service discovery and verification
boundary_mappings.dba_route: External discovery and identity input adjacent to POP; DQ-006 remains open
boundary_mappings.allowed_interpretation: A reference for DNS-based discovery, externally verifiable names, and identity metadata.
boundary_mappings.forbidden_promotion: Discovery or external identity metadata must not be promoted to DBOS Digital Entity identity, POP persona continuity, Capability, Permission, registration, or admission.

boundary_mappings.dba_route: Competitive and design reference for policy, sandbox, registry, and audit surfaces
boundary_mappings.allowed_interpretation: A source-pinned comparison input after release-state correction.
boundary_mappings.forbidden_promotion: Main-branch feature declarations, audit logs, sandbox controls, or registry records must not be copied as adopted capability or used as DBA implementation, maturity, or production evidence.

boundary_mappings.dba_route: Future PR-G2 and PR-G4 conformance review inputs
boundary_mappings.allowed_interpretation: A research input supporting final-state, invariant, contract, and authority-effect assertions in addition to exception detection.
boundary_mappings.forbidden_promotion: The preprint must not be promoted to a universal defect rate, an implemented validator, executed conformance, a gate result, or production evidence.

gap_actions.artifact: FINOS telemetry versus offline-verifiable receipt comparison
One-page FINOS or conference discussion note
